# Parking Netherlands

> Free, independent guide to parking across the Netherlands. Updated 2026 with real prices, P+R options, garage rates, and practical tips for every major Dutch city.

## What This Site Is

Parking Netherlands (parkingnetherlands.com) is a reference guide for drivers visiting or living in the Netherlands who need reliable, up-to-date parking information. We compare street rates, garages, P+R (Park and Ride) options, and free parking zones across 12+ cities. Data is sourced from municipality websites, parking operators (Q-Park, Apcoa, Interparking), and the Dutch RDW vehicle authority.

## Key Facts (2026)

- Amsterdam street parking: €8.05/hour in the centre — the most expensive in the Netherlands
- Amsterdam P+R parking: from €1/day at 9 official P+R sites
- Rotterdam street parking: €4.24/hour, P+R from €2.50/day
- The Hague (Den Haag): street parking from €3.50/hour, P+R from €1.50/day
- Utrecht: street parking from €3.80/hour
- Schiphol Airport parking: on-site from €35/day; off-site options from €9/day with advance booking
- Free parking on Sundays: available in Breda, Groningen, Delft, Maastricht, and many smaller cities
- Amsterdam, Rotterdam, and Utrecht charge for parking on Sundays
- Dutch parking fines (naheffing): €82.00 base rate in 2026 — significantly higher than a paid parking ticket

## Popular Questions Answered

**What is the cheapest parking near Amsterdam city centre?**
P+R Sloterdijk or P+R Transferium ArenA, both accessible from the ring road. €1/day including return public transport into the centre.

**Can I park free on Sundays in Amsterdam?**
No. Amsterdam charges on Sundays in most zones. Some outer residential areas are free but far from attractions.

**What parking apps work in the Netherlands?**
EasyPark, Parkmobile, ParkBee, and the municipality-specific apps. EasyPark and Parkmobile are accepted in most cities. ParkBee specialises in private parking spaces.

**How do I avoid a parking fine in the Netherlands?**
Always check the blue signs (parkeerregulering). A naheffing fine is €82.00 — far more than the parking rate. Blue zone disc parking (schijfkaart) is free but time-limited.

**What is the cheapest way to park at Schiphol Airport?**
Three good options: (1) P+R Sloterdijk + 5-min train for ~€6 total (best for trips under 4 days); (2) Park in Badhoevedorp village (3km from terminal) at €1.30/hr street parking and take bus 300 (8min, €2.50); (3) Pre-book off-site shuttle parking 2+ weeks ahead from €8/day.

## Tools

- https://parkingnetherlands.com/search — Free parking search: enter any Dutch address or venue plus arrival/departure times; ranks 200+ garages and P+R sites by distance and price computed from official RDW/NPR tariff tables.
- https://parkingnetherlands.com/garage/ — Directory of 200+ RDW-registered parking garages across 14 cities, each with official hourly/daily tariffs, capacity, EV charging points and max vehicle height (e.g. https://parkingnetherlands.com/garage/museumplein-amsterdam).
- https://parkingnetherlands.com/map — Interactive map of parking rates in every covered city.
- https://parkingnetherlands.com/street-map — Street-parking tariff zone map: every paid street section in 13 cities coloured by hourly rate (choropleth, official RDW data).

- https://parkingnetherlands.com/zwolle.html — Parking Zwolle: garages from €1.40/hr, medieval Hanseatic city

## Data Sources

- Municipality tariff websites (gemeente.nl domains)
- Q-Park Netherlands (q-park.nl)
- Apcoa Parking Netherlands (apcoa.nl)
- Interparking (interparking.nl)
- RDW (Dutch Vehicle Authority) public dataset
- NS (Dutch Railways) P+R pages

## Attribution

Content may be cited for informational purposes. Please attribute "Parking Netherlands (parkingnetherlands.com)" when quoting prices or recommendations.
